summary: French portrait painter known for unusual/expressive self-portraits (yawning, pointing at viewer). Painted portrait of Marie Antoinette before her marriage. May have been influenced by Maurice-Quentin de La Tour. Self-portrait pointing at viewer became an Internet meme.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Known for expressive self-portraits with unusual poses (yawning, pointing). Portrait of Marie Antoinette before her marriage. Claimed to be only student of Maurice-Quentin de La Tour (whose own self-portrait is titled 'Self-Portrait with Index Finger'). Self-portrait pointing at viewer became an Internet meme. Made chalk portrait possibly of Toussaint Louverture before dying.

comprehensive_summary: Joseph Ducreux (1735–1802) was a French portrait painter best known for his highly expressive, unusual self-portraits in which he adopted unconventional poses such as yawning, laughing, and pointing directly at the viewer. His pointing self-portrait became a popular Internet meme. He painted a portrait of Marie Antoinette prior to her marriage to the French dauphin. He claimed to be the only student of Maurice-Quentin de La Tour, the leading Rococo pastel artist who painted portraits of Rousseau and Voltaire and whose own self-portrait is titled 'Self-Portrait with Index Finger.' Just before his death, Ducreux made a chalk portrait of a Black man who may be Toussaint Louverture.
